Title: Property types
Summary: Seven property types: text, multiline, number, boolean, image, ref, tags.
Body:
## The list

- **text**: single line input. Use for short labels.
- **multiline**: textarea. Use for paragraphs or notes.
- **number**: numeric input. JSON stores it as a number; empty input becomes `undefined`, not `0`.
- **boolean**: checkbox.
- **image**: an absolute filesystem path to an image. Uses the same [AssetPicker](/help/assets-and-image-editor/click-vs-right-click) as the rest of the app, so right click also gets you Edit / Duplicate / Delete on the picked image.
- **ref**: a string id pointing into another domain. Pick a RefDomain (`npc`, `item`, `quest`, `faction`, `dialog`, `balloon`) when you define the property; the entry form then autocompletes from the right catalog.
- **tags**: a list of strings. The chip control commits a tag on :kbd[Comma] or :kbd[Enter], and :kbd[Backspace] on an empty draft removes the last chip.

## Defaults

Each property carries an optional DefaultValue. When you create a new entry, its Properties bag is pre populated from the schema's defaults, so the form starts in a sensible state instead of blank everywhere.

## Reserved entry ids

`_meta` and `_layout` are reserved. The first is the schema file. The second is reserved for future per category layout state. Trying to save an entry with either id fails server side.
